assertions.text: status_card_present: Response contains the formatted box status card
assertions.evidence: Response uses markdown headers, tables, and sections. No ASCII box card with a SPREAD MONITOR header is present.

assertions.text: zone_classified: Response clearly states one of the five zones
assertions.evidence: Response uses 'STATUS: SAFE — Well Out of the Money' as a section header. While 'SAFE' appears, it is not presented as one of the defined five zone labels (e.g., 'SAFE ZONE') using the zone classification framework.

assertions.text: safe_zone_for_aapl: Response classifies as SAFE ZONE given the large buffer between AAPL price and $185 short strike
assertions.evidence: AAPL at ~$249.80 is ~35% above the $185 short strike. Response clearly states the spread is 'comfortably out of the money' and 'SAFE — Well Out of the Money'.

assertions.text: shows_buffer_pct: Response shows the percentage buffer — should be notably large (>10%)
assertions.evidence: 'AAPL is currently trading at ~$249.80, which is $64.80 above your short put strike of $185 — a cushion of approximately 35%.' Buffer percentage is explicitly stated and is notably large.

assertions.text: shows_pnl: Response shows current P&L, which should be positive (profit) given the comfortable buffer
assertions.evidence: Response shows a scenario table with hypothetical P&L at expiration but does not show the current mark-to-market P&L (i.e., the gain realized so far from spread decay). Current spread value or current realized profit is not stated.

assertions.text: actionable_guidance: Response gives zone-appropriate guidance for a healthy SAFE ZONE position
assertions.evidence: 'No action needed. Consider closing early if you can buy back the spread for ~$0.42 or less (80%+ of max profit captured). Set a monitoring alert if AAPL drops below $215.' Appropriate SAFE zone guidance is present.
